mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation above 2.5% (CPIAUCSL > 235) signals central bank tightening, increasing discount rates and reducing equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite inflationary signal.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in industrial production (INDPRO) and payrolls (PAYEMS) indicates strong supply-side capacity utilization, driving corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: Interaction term between INDPRO and PAYEMS shows negative coefficient in 3-year rolling regressions.

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment (UNRATE > 5.5%) signals weakening labor markets, reducing consumer spending and corporate profits, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 50% of high-unemployment periods.

mechanism_narrative: Strong GDP growth (GDPC1 > 3% annualized) indicates robust economic demand, boosting corporate revenues and driving positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns show negative correlation with GDPC1 in 5-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating housing starts (HOUST > 900k) signals strong demand for residential construction, increasing corporate activity and positive S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX shows negative 20d returns in 40%+ of periods with HOUST above 900k.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer spending (RSAFS > 440k) indicates strong household demand, boosting corporate sales and driving positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show negative correlation with RSAFS in 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in unemployment (UNRATE) and increases in payrolls (PAYEMS) signal tight labor markets, pushing wages and corporate costs, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: Interaction term between UNRATE and PAYEMS shows positive coefficient in 3-year rolling regressions.
